steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The passage states that a meta‑analysis showed a minimum sample size of 176 individuals is needed to trust a finding of population‑level handedness.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Choice D points out that the sample size behind the 1991 claim is far below the 176‑person threshold, which matches the reason to treat the claim skeptically.

The other three either introduce unrelated details, misstate the gorilla study, or confuse frequency with sample size.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
